title: `quiver:status` Command
objective: Ship `npx create-quiver status` that prints a single-screen dashboard of active specs, in-progress slices, open PRs (optional), and the next up suggestion.
description: The status command reads every spec's STATUS.md, every slice's status field, the current git worktree list, and optionally GitHub PRs via `gh`. Without `gh`, the command omits the PR section and prints a short note. The output must fit in ~20 lines so it is usable at the start of every session. JSON mode is the stable machine contract.
must: Create `src/create-quiver/lib/git-state.js` that reads worktrees (`git worktree list --porcelain`), current branches, and optionally PRs via `gh`
Detect `gh` with a cross-platform probe; when absent, omit the PR section and print `(gh not installed — PR section skipped)`
Print the Next-Up suggestion using `quiver:plan --only-ready` output
Add a `docs/COMMANDS.md` row and update `README.md` npm scripts list
not_included: Installing or auto-detecting `gh` authentication (respect existing config)
acceptance: `npx create-quiver status` prints active specs, in-progress slices, and a Next-Up line
On a machine without `gh`, the command completes with exit 0 and omits the PR section with a note
`npx create-quiver status --json` returns valid JSON with keys `active_specs`, `in_progress`, `open_prs`, `next_up`
`npx create-quiver status --no-pr` skips `gh` even when installed
tests: node -e "const s=require('/tmp/quiver-status.json'); if(!('active_specs' in s)) process.exit(1)"
assumptions: `gh auth status` is a reliable proxy for `gh` availability
`git worktree list --porcelain` output is stable across supported git versions
Less than 20 lines of output is acceptable for the expected repo sizes
